zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Meynell於1359年出生在英國德比夏爾紐哈爾時，她的父親Richard de Meynell25歲，母親Joan de Kirk19歲。她於1388年與Thomas Clinton在英國瓦維其可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1457年在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾巴登享年98歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Meynell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 Norman, English: locative name from one of the French places named Mesnil, from Old French mesnil (Latin * mansionile, a diminutive of mansio ‘abode, habitation’), used of the country domain where a noble lived with his family and retainers. As a surname it may also be used of a member of the household, retainer. Some of the bearers listed below may belong to (2).
2 English: relationship name from the Continental Germanic female personal name Maginhild.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Meynell was born in 1359, in Newhall, Derbyshire, England, her father, Richard de Meynell, was 25 and her mother, Joan de Kirk, was 19. She married Thomas Clinton in 1388, in Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1457, in Bardon, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 98.

Elle a épousé Thomas Clinton en 1388, à Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1457, à Bardon, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 98 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Meynellが1359年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアニューハルで生まれた時，父Richard de Meynellは25歳，母Joan de Kirkは19歳であった。1388年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアでThomas Clintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1457年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアバードンで98歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Meynell(이)가 1359년 영국 더비실르 뉴홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Meynell(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Kirk(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1388년 영국 워윅실르에서 Thomas Clinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1457년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 바든에서 98세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Clinton em 1388, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Meynell родилась в 1359 г. в местности нюхолл, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard de Meynell было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Kirk, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Clinton в 1388 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1457 г. в местности бардон, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 98 лет.
